Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=L-tryptophan + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = 2-methyl-L-tryptophan + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:17321, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:57912, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:85908; EC=2.1.1.106; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:23064318};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in the biosynthetic pathway of the antibiotic thiostrepton A. First, TsrM catalyzes the transfer of a methyl group from S-adenosyl methionine (SAM) to cobalamin, leading to the formation of methylcobalamin (CH3-cobalamin) and S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine (SAH). Then the methyl group is transferred to the C2 position of tryptophan (Trp) with the concerted action of the radical SAM [4Fe-4S] center, leading to the production of methyltryptophan. {ECO:0000269|PubMed:19246004, ECO:0000269|PubMed:19265401, ECO:0000269|PubMed:23064318}.
